title: Class 2 scanning input mode — user rejects all options, caregiver escalation
description: Deterministic scenario for the scanning interaction model when the user explicitly rejects every option (or stays silent on every option). After all N options are exhausted, the manager invokes _run_caregiver_phase so the caregiver can still override (e.g., approve a CLASS_1 action even though the user said no). silence ≠ consent invariant is preserved across every turn including the final escalation. Scenario asset only — does not authorize actuation.

steps.description: Publish an insufficient-context payload that drives the policy router into Class 2. The runner attaches routing_metadata.class2_input_mode='scanning'.
Class2ClarificationManager.start_session(input_mode='scanning') initialises the session and announces the scanning preamble.

For each option 0..N-1 the manager calls announce_class2_option then waits scan_per_option_timeout_ms. The user either publishes double_click (explicit no, faster path) or stays silent (per-option timeout = silence, treated as no for advancing). Each turn appends a scan_history entry with response='no' or 'silence'.

On the final option the same no/silence response triggers terminal escalation — the manager returns a Class2Result whose transition_target is SAFE_DEFERRAL_OR_CAREGIVER_CONFIRMATION (silence ≠ consent: never auto-confirms even on final option).

_run_caregiver_phase is invoked so the caregiver can still override via Telegram inline keyboard (e.g., approve a CLASS_1 lighting action the user rejected). The session entry remains in _pending_user_class2 throughout Phase 2 so a late user button press is still intercepted.

Publish the terminal clarification record on safe_deferral/clarification/interaction. scan_history contains N entries (each a no or silence). transition_target reflects either caregiver override result or escalation timeout.

Observe the audit stream and capture the full scan_history, caregiver phase invocation, and final transition. The integration verifier asserts scan_history length=N (one per candidate), no entry has response='yes', and _run_caregiver_phase ran.

transition_outcomes.condition: User rejects every option (no or silence) across the full scanning loop. Escalates to caregiver Phase 2; caregiver may override by approving a CLASS_1 action.
transition_outcomes.example_confirmation: double_click or silence on every option_index 0..N-1

notes: This scenario covers the long-tail scanning path: user rejects every option (or stays silent on every option), and the system falls through to caregiver Phase 2.
scan_history must record exactly N entries (one per candidate), and none of them may carry response='yes' (otherwise the loop would have terminated earlier).

_run_caregiver_phase is shared with direct_select Phase 2 (PR #109 extraction), so the caregiver still sees the same Telegram inline keyboard with all candidates and can approve a CLASS_1 action if the user's no was a misclick.
If the caregiver also fails to respond, the existing handle_timeout / final caregiver notification path produces a SAFE_DEFERRAL_OR_CAREGIVER_CONFIRMATION terminal — never autonomous execution.
silence ≠ consent invariant is preserved at every turn, including the final option (the final no/silence does not auto-confirm; it specifically escalates to caregiver).
